mentions.note: Long knives out for Paychex unfairly. Stock at $17 with 4.5% yield is good value. Cramer not sticking neck out but company shouldn't be this slow. Good for long-term hold.

Payroll processor under pressure from AI/Anthropic/OpenAI fears. Yield (4.7%) has been support level before but not reliable now with so many stocks yielding 5–6%. Shorts vs. longs shadow boxing; hard to tell who wins.

Q3 beats on revenue (20% growth) and earnings (22% op income growth); 27% cash flow growth; AI disruption fears overblown; down 44% from June highs despite solid fundamentals; 17x forward earnings with ~5% yield.
Payroll software seen as AI-vulnerable by market; Cramer notes similar disenfranchisement narrative as ADP despite need for unemployment/tax compliance.
Good yield, can make a comeback; preferred alternative to Manpower.
